# The case for narrow AI in solo-dev tooling

## Core Argument
*(What is the single biggest claim this newsletter is making?)*

Solo developers ship faster with narrow, predictable AI tools (code-review agents, commit-message generators) than with broad LLM chat — because the surface area of failure modes is bounded and the cost of recovery is low.

## Why I Saved This
*(Why does this matter to me right now?)*

Aligns with himmel's plugin-marketplace philosophy.
